mnemonics.title: Page Replacement Steps
mnemonics.content: FIND-FREE-WRITE-BRING-UPDATE-RESTART (FF-W-BUR): Find page, Find frame/Select victim, Write if dirty, Bring new page, Update tables, Restart instruction

mnemonics.title: Belady's Anomaly
mnemonics.content: FIFO Fails Increasing Frames: Only FIFO suffers from increased page faults when given more frames

mnemonics.title: Algorithm Order
mnemonics.content: OPT Over LRU, LRU Over FIFO (OOLF): Optimal > LRU > FIFO in efficiency

mnemonics.title: Dirty Bit Check
mnemonics.content: MODIFIED? MUST WRITE! (MMW): If dirty bit set, page must be written to disk before replacement

acronyms.usage: Theoretical best algorithm requiring future knowledge
Replaces page not used for longest time in past

acronyms.expansion: Circular Linked Organization of Page Frames
acronyms.usage: Remember circular buffer structure with reference bit checks
Replaces oldest page regardless of usage frequency

visualTips.visualization: Imagine pages as people in a cafeteria line - first person in gets replaced first
Visualize pages on a timeline - farthest left (oldest) gets replaced
Picture a circular clock face with pages. The 'hand' sweeps checking reference bits (1→0, 0→replace)
Imagine adding more shelves (frames) but needing to rearrange books (pages) more frequently

analogies.analogy: Like a library with limited study tables - least productive user gets replaced by new arrivals
A chess grandmaster who always makes perfect future-aware moves
Supermarket shelf stocking - least popular items get replaced by new products
Night watchman making rounds - checks each room (page) in order, evicts first inactive occupant
